title: Modals of Obligation & Advice
description: Say what is necessary, forbidden, optional or wise with must, have to, mustn't, don't have to and should — and master the classic contrast between mustn't (forbidden) and don't have to (not necessary).
sources: CEFR B1 descriptors (Council of Europe, Common European Framework of Reference)
Raymond Murphy, English Grammar in Use (must and have to; mustn't and don't have to; should)
